key_insight: A dual-output physics-informed residual neural network can jointly predict hot-deformation flow stress and dynamic recrystallization in a Mo-rich titanium alloy while enforcing constitutive physics.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more accurate and interpretable constitutive model for designing thermomechanical processing windows in advanced titanium alloys.
method: Trained stacked residual physics-informed neural networks on experimental flow-stress curves from 800–1050 °C and 0.01–10 s⁻¹.
Embedded thermomechanical constraints such as strain hardening, thermal softening, strain-rate sensitivity, and post-peak softening via automatic differentiation.
Added DRX-aware outputs with JMAK-Avrami regularization, DRX saturation constraints, and Arrhenius consistency to link recrystallization fraction to stress.
evidence_signal: The DRX-aware STAR-PINN achieved RMSE = 11.69 MPa, MAE = 4.83 MPa, R² = 0.9850, and cross-validated RMSE = 12.47 ± 0.26 MPa while reproducing flow curves, DRX kinetics, and Zener-Hollomon trends.
watch_item: Performance depends on well-tuned physics regularization and may be less reliable when extrapolating beyond the tested temperature/strain-rate range.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Residual Deep Learning for Constitutive Modeling of Hot Deformation and Dynamic Recrystallization in a Mo-Rich $α+β$ Titanium Alloy
